title: GitHub vs GitLab
description: Compare GitHub and GitLab across repositories, CI/CD, code review, AI features, and DevOps toolchain completeness for development teams.
verdict: GitHub wins for most development teams thanks to its unmatched open-source community, extensive third-party integration marketplace, and GitHub Copilot which is the leading AI coding assistant. GitHub Actions provides powerful CI/CD that rivals GitLab's built-in pipelines, and GitHub's mobile app and social coding features foster better collaboration. GitLab stands out for teams that need a complete, single-application DevOps lifecycle with built-in CI/CD from the start, review apps, and a robust self-hosted option for enterprises with strict compliance needs. For most public and private projects, GitHub's community and ecosystem are difficult to beat.

faqs.question: Can I migrate from GitHub to GitLab or vice versa?
faqs.answer: Both platforms provide import and export tools that support direct repository migration including issues, pull requests, and wiki content. GitHub offers a GitHub Importer tool for GitLab, while GitLab provides project import from GitHub with comprehensive mapping of metadata.

faqs.question: Which platform has better CI/CD capabilities?
faqs.answer: GitLab offers a more mature built-in CI/CD with native Kubernetes integration and review apps, requiring no external services. GitHub Actions has grown rapidly and offers superior third-party action marketplace, better Windows/macOS runner support, and more flexible workflow configurations.

faqs.question: How does pricing compare for private repositories?
faqs.answer: GitHub offers free private repositories with up to 2000 minutes of Actions per month for individuals. GitLab's free tier also includes private repos and up to 400 CI/CD minutes. Both scale with team size, but GitLab's premium tiers often provide more DevOps features at lower price points.

faqs.question: Which is better for enterprise self-hosting?
faqs.answer: GitLab is widely considered the better option for self-hosting with GitLab Self-Managed providing the full DevOps platform on your own infrastructure. GitHub Enterprise Server is also available but requires more infrastructure management and lacks some features found in the cloud version.
